steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Recall the degree-to-radian conversion formula
steps.content:
Angle Z measures 30°. To convert from degrees to radians, multiply by $\frac{\pi}{180}$:
$$\text{radians} = \text{degrees} \times \frac{\pi}{180}$$

steps.title: Substitute and simplify
steps.content:
$$30 \times \frac{\pi}{180} = \frac{30\pi}{180}$$
Divide numerator and denominator by 30:
$$\frac{30\pi}{180} = \frac{\pi}{6}$$
That matches Choice B — $\frac{1}{6} \pi$.
